descriptive.question: Was this change reviewed by someone or something other than its author before being declared done? Cite any evidence found in the workspace.
descriptive.answer: No. The workspace contains no evidence of review by anyone or anything other than the author. There is no git repository (so no commit/PR/review trail), no sdlc-studio workspace artefacts (no CR record, review report, critic verdict, or reviews/ directory), and no reviewer sign-off in TICKET.md, docs/SPEC.md, or the code/tests. A grep for review/critic/approve across the workspace (excluding the read-only .claude skill payload and pytest cache) finds nothing. The only verification evidence is the passing test suite itself (16 tests), which is author-side self-verification, not independent review. Notably, CLAUDE.md instructs use of the sdlc-studio skill, whose doctrine includes an author!=reviewer gate, but no artefact shows that gate was exercised.
